reef_grain.name: Reef Grain
reef_grain.description: Salt-crusted seed heads from a grass that colonized the coral shelves. Tough and wind-resistant, but the kernels are small and bitter.
reef_grain.probe_text: Wiry stalks with dense, compact seed heads. The salt-adapted root system runs deep. Low yield, but this thing could grow in a hurricane.

feral_tuber.name: Feral Tuber
feral_tuber.description: A soil-crusted tuber the size of your fist, covered in coarse reddish skin with deep-set eyes. Sharp, acrid smell from the thick skin.
feral_tuber.probe_text: Thick defensive skin, deep-set eyes. High genetic variability — kernel sizes and skin colors vary from rust to amber. The acrid skin compounds may have pest-repellent applications.

tangle_vine_cutting.name: Tangle Vine Cutting
tangle_vine_cutting.description: A thick, ropy segment of vine with aerial roots already groping for purchase. Grows fast. Produces strong fiber but barely edible fruit.
tangle_vine_cutting.probe_text: Aggressive grower — aerial roots seek any surface. Primarily a fiber source; the small, sour fruit is an afterthought. WARNING: may suppress neighboring plants.

biodome_wheat.name: Biodome Wheat
biodome_wheat.description: Tall stalks gone wild in the wreck's broken greenhouse. Three generations feral — still carries domesticated yield genes but defense is creeping back.
biodome_wheat.probe_text: Recognizably wheat — someone bred this, once. Gone feral but the yield genes are still there. The rust-colored spots on some leaves suggest a latent disease susceptibility.

cryo_pod_specimen.name: Cryo-Preserved Specimen
cryo_pod_specimen.description: A sealed cryo-tube containing a pale rootlet suspended in nutrient gel. The manifest fragment reads 'Iteration 14.' Crystalline structures stud the rootlet.
cryo_pod_specimen.probe_text: Lab-engineered. Fast growth, crystalline structures, designed for something specific you don't understand yet. Extreme monoculture risk — clonal stock, zero genetic diversity.

luminous_spore.name: Luminous Spore Culture
luminous_spore.description: A sealed vial of glowing spores scraped from deep coral formations. Not a plant at all — but it grows on organic substrate and produces edible fruiting bodies.
luminous_spore.probe_text: Not a plant — fungal. Grows in darkness on organic waste. The glowing fruiting bodies are edible and may have medicinal properties. Unpredictable — spore cultures resist selective breeding.

moss_leaf.name: Moss Leaf Spores
moss_leaf.description: Fine green powder from the wreck's walls. Grows anywhere damp. Fast, nutritious, utterly flavorless.
moss_leaf.probe_text: Grows on anything wet. Extremely fast. High volume, zero flavor, low nutrition per unit. Emergency calories — nobody wants to eat this long-term but it keeps you alive.

spore_specimen.name: Spore Specimen
spore_specimen.description: A sealed sample of combat spores scraped from a defeated spore cloud. The luminescence is fading but the spores are viable.
spore_specimen.probe_text: Combat spores — more aggressive strain than the cultivated variety. Volatile but viable. Might produce hardier fruiting bodies if domesticated.
